scope: z_* exclusion consistency across backfill, memory-index, code-graph, and description discovery
summary: z_future backfill skip already shipped this session. The residual is real: exclusion policy is split across four tooling paths and is not authoritative. z_archive divergence between memory and code-graph is by-design, not a bug.

title: Authoritative z_* spec-tree exclusion helper
problem: Exclusion policy is duplicated, not authoritative. Memory scope excludes z_future and external but not z_archive. Code graph excludes both z_future and z_archive. Spec discovery skips only scratch, memory, node_modules. Backfill now skips z_future via its own local set.
evidence: EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY index-scope.ts:187-190, EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH :192-200, shouldDescendSpecDiscoveryDirectory spec-doc-paths.ts:117-123, backfill EXCLUDED_DIRS backfill-graph-metadata.ts:27
proposal: Add one exported helper, e.g. isExcludedSpecTreeSegment or shouldDescendGeneratedMetadataDirectory, and use it at traversal boundaries in the generator and discovery paths. Keep memory z_archive inclusion by-design via a separate generatedMetadata policy.

title: z_future backfill skip already shipped (verify residual only)
problem: The seats found z_future already excluded in current backfill source. This is the fix landed this session. The remaining work is the inconsistency across siblings, not re-fixing backfill.
evidence: EXCLUDED_DIRS backfill-graph-metadata.ts:27 includes z_future, ARCHIVE_SEGMENT_RE :28, dist twin scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js:18
proposal: Mark z_future backfill exclusion DONE. Add a regression test asserting backfill skips z_future, and update the stale graph README that still says future trees are included at scripts/graph/README.md:18.

title: Apply the shared guard to descriptions.json discovery
problem: The global description cache scanner has its own SCAN_SKIP_DIRECTORIES that omits z_* and external, so it can enter staging or archive folders that other paths exclude.
evidence: SCAN_SKIP_DIRECTORIES folder-discovery.ts:94-103, discoverSpecFolders walks non-skipped dirs :325-337 and recurses :371
proposal: Replace local SCAN_SKIP_DIRECTORIES with the authoritative spec-tree exclusion helper from a2-f1, optionally adding a scoped cache mode.

title: Decide memory archive policy explicitly (do not mix)
problem: Memory indexing descends z_archive by design and tests assert it. This is intentional decay-design behavior, deprioritized by ARCHIVE_MULTIPLIERS 0.1, not a bug to silently change.
evidence: EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY comment index-scope.ts:183-186 documents the intent, full-spec-doc-indexing.vitest.ts:282-292 asserts z_archive accepted z_future rejected
proposal: Keep memory archive retrieval. Split policies so generated-JSON tools use a stricter generatedMetadata policy while memory_index_scan keeps archive retrieval. Do not extend memory exclusion to z_archive.
